title: Companies Act 1967 — official overview
summary: ACRA describes the Companies Act 1967 as the principal legislation governing company incorporation, management, operations, director duties and winding-up procedures in Singapore.

title: Choosing company directors and key officers
summary: ACRA guidance states that a local company must have at least one director who meets Singapore residency requirements. Directors must also satisfy age, capacity and disqualification requirements.

title: Preparing or adopting a company constitution
summary: ACRA explains that a company constitution contains rules for operating the company and addresses matters such as the company name, activities, shareholder liability and share capital.

title: Companies Act 1967 — registered office
summary: The Companies Act requires a company to maintain a registered office in Singapore for official communications. The current statutory text and any applicable exceptions must be checked on Singapore Statutes Online.

title: Companies Act 1967 — accounting records
summary: The Companies Act contains requirements for companies to keep accounting and related records that explain transactions and financial position and support the preparation of financial statements.

title: Updating company information, officers and shareholders
summary: ACRA publishes guidance on notifying changes to company information, officers, shares and shareholders. Filing deadlines and requirements depend on the specific change and should be checked on the current ACRA page.
